title: Sweet Sticky Thai Chinese Cabbage Khanom Thai
description: A floral and chewy Thai dish featuring Chinese cabbage and a sweet glaze, perfect for a hangover cure. This no-onion-no-garlic-friendly recipe is served in a khanom Thai form on a lazy Susan spinner.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the sugar, water, rice vinegar, and salt, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the glaze has thickened.
In a large skillet or wok, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Add the Chinese cabbage leaves to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they are slightly wilted.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and sesame oil.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the glaze and stir to combine.
Pour the glaze over the Chinese cabbage and toss to coat.
Arrange the cabbage leaves in a khanom Thai form on a lazy Susan spinner.
